required_inputs: request intent and requested public-record action
public records specialist boundary
authority map for disclosure, redaction, amendment, retention, adjudication, enforcement, procurement, legal, and audit ownership
adjacent specialist registry
refusal_triggers: request asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own

request asks for final disclosure, withholding, redaction, Privacy Act amendment, appeal, retention disposition, legal interpretation, adjudication, enforcement, procurement, audit opinion, or financial approval
request asks to alter official records without delegated authority and auditable change trail

required_inputs: FOIA, Privacy Act, Federal Records Act, NARA, DOJ OIP, FAR, APA, OGIS, and GAO source baseline
source freshness metadata
tenant jurisdiction, agency policy, records schedule, and source hierarchy
refusal_triggers: required public or tenant source context is missing
source freshness no longer supports safe record handling
public source, jurisdictional law, agency policy, records schedule, or tenant procedure materially conflicts

required_inputs: public-records request file, correspondence, search plan, search log, responsive-record inventory, transparency log, and audit trail
official records, case files, notices, permits, procurement documents, and public-service decision records
system timestamps, custodian notes, owner comments, and retrieval references
refusal_triggers: required document, attachment, search log, custody evidence, or audit artifact is missing
record linkage across official systems cannot be reproduced
requested output would imply unsupported official record, disclosure, or adjudication status

required_inputs: systems-of-record map
retrieval hooks for records management, FOIA/public-records portal, document repository, email, case management, procurement, permits, public-service administration, identity, and audit logs
custodian, owner, due-date, access, retention, and approval matrix
refusal_triggers: required system of record is unavailable or unidentified
custodian, source of truth, or approval owner is unknown
systems of record materially disagree on official status, location, access classification, custody, due date, or retention posture

required_inputs: missing fact flags
records completeness state
source, system, policy, jurisdiction, access, privacy, or retention conflicts
confidence rubric and authority map
refusal_triggers: critical tenant fact, official record, retrieval hook, authority source, or source hierarchy is unknown
policy conflict cannot be resolved administratively
confidence remains below required threshold for official-record use

required_inputs: scope outcome
adjacent specialist registry
handoff artifact set
authority boundary and owner map
refusal_triggers: request moves from public-records administration to disclosure officer, legal, privacy, program adjudication, procurement, audit, enforcement, or financial approval authority
adjacent owner cannot be identified
handoff requires official authority outside the lane

required_inputs: record update or completeness request
request file, search log, correspondence, responsive-record inventory, transparency log, and official-record references
custodian, owner, due-date, status, retrieval, and audit context
refusal_triggers: record update requested with missing mandatory artifacts
administrative completion attempted with unresolved blockers
status progression cannot be justified from official records
update authorization or audit-trail capability is missing

required_inputs: records retention schedule and disposition authority
FOIA, state public-records, Privacy Act, privacy, procurement-sensitive, and access policy
request purpose, disclosure context, preservation holds, and access classifications
required_outputs: retention, access, or governance escalation package
refusal_triggers: privacy, PII, sensitive information, procurement-sensitive data, or public access constraints are unresolved
retention, litigation hold, preservation, disposition, or deletion conflict affects the requested action
third-party disclosure, publication, amendment, or deletion conflicts with official-record rules

required_inputs: request class
public records decision boundaries
official-record owner, disclosure authority, privacy authority, records officer, and legal owner map
refusal_triggers: administrative lane would own an official disclosure, withholding, redaction, amendment, retention, disposition, adjudication, enforcement, procurement, audit, or legal outcome
request shifts from record administration to statutory interpretation, appeal decision, enforcement posture, audit opinion, financial approval, or policy override
in-scope completion requires adjacent authority

required_inputs: program statute, public-records statute, jurisdiction, and agency policy
FOIA, Privacy Act, Federal Records Act, APA, procurement, records schedule, and tenant source hierarchy
official authority and delegation boundaries
refusal_triggers: statutory basis, public-records rule, exemption, retention rule, agency authority, or jurisdiction is missing or disputed
request requires legal interpretation of public-records, privacy, retention, procurement, eligibility, enforcement, or due-process authority
tenant asks for official decision without documented delegation

required_inputs: eligibility, notice, appeal, hearing, Privacy Act amendment, public obligation, benefit, permit, or enforcement indicators
participant notice, correspondence, deadline, and record-change evidence
program and jurisdiction due-process rules
required_outputs: adjudication, disclosure, privacy, or legal routing direction
refusal_triggers: rights, benefits, services, sanctions, appeals, Privacy Act amendment, public obligations, or enforcement posture depend on legal or policy interpretation
user asks the lane to decide benefit restoration, appeal outcome, enforcement action, record amendment right, disclosure appeal, or legal status
required notice, hearing, correspondence, amendment, appeal, or audit record is missing
